title: France's national political crisis is taking root locally
subhed: The first round of the municipal elections, marked by low turnout and fragmented landscapes in big cities, confirms the depth of France's political crisis, writes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

title: US military says hit Iran missile sites near Strait of Hormuz with 'bunker buster' bombs
subhed: Central Command announced the military action in a statement on X.

title: Iran confirms death of security chief Larijani
subhed: Iran's Supreme National Security Council confirmed the death of 'Martyr Dr. Ali Larijani,' stating that his son and his bodyguards died with him, after Israel announced earlier on Tuesday it had killed him in an air strike.

title: PSG crush Chelsea to reach Champions League quarter-finals
subhed: Thanks to the 3-0 win on Tuesday, Paris Saint-Germain's dream of retaining the Champions League title remains very much alive, a year after winning the trophy for the first time in their history.

title: Violence in restaurant kitchens exposes deep wounds in fine dining
subhed: The recent allegations against renowned Danish chef René Redzepi highlight the urgent need for a profound overhaul of management practices in the restaurant industry. Initiatives are spreading in France to break the silence surrounding abuse in the sector.

title: French court examines Yazidi genocide for first time in trial of jihadist
subhed: Sabri Essid is being tried in absentia, charged with 'genocide,' a first in France. Two women from the Iraqi minority will testify, recounting how they were enslaved.

title: Ravens use memory, not predators, to find kills
subhed: A study conducted in Yellowstone National Park in the United States finds that corvids return to the sites of previous feasts thanks to sophisticated strategies that had completely eluded researchers until now.
